key_insight: LASKO treats skill editing as a Lie algebroid problem, using the anchor to separate visible Markdown changes from latent structure and the bracket to detect noncommuting edits.
engineering_relevance: This could make agent self-improvement cheaper and safer by filtering likely edit conflicts in microseconds before running expensive model-based validation.
method: Model typed, anchored Markdown skills as the base category.
Represent edit policies as sections of a controlled Lie algebroid with anchor \u03c1.
Use bracket screening to test whether edits commute before rollout/validation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports preliminary benchmark gains, including nearly 15x speedup on a causal extraction task versus brute-force validation with a DeepSeek V3.1 4-bit 671B model.
watch_item: The results are preliminary, so the practical value depends on whether the Lie-bracket screening remains reliable across diverse skill-editing workflows and real production templates.
paper_title: Agentic Skill Optimization over Lie Algebroids
